goal: Find one or more new orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work where active-signal-v1@1 is absent, prove their additive value, and avoid repeating v1's single-filter event-entry mistakes.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable: no retuning of hours, days, thresholds, or execution rules.
Raw single-filter event-entry constructions (H1/H2 style) are permanently banned.
Every new candidate must first pass complement-first mode before any other evaluation.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction Wave A is fully exhausted.
Each prototype must have at least three logical layers: new information layer, regime/state confirmation, and entry-density limiter.
A candidate whose profit vanishes under strict-additive mode is rejected regardless of permissive-integrated performance.

stages.title: Postmortem plan_v1 and fix new rules
stages.objective: Record the specific lessons from the failed v1 cycle so the agent does not repeat the same mistakes under different names, and codify the invariant rules for v2.
actions: Open a new research_project for the v2 cycle.

Write a postmortem in research_record: H1/H2 failed due to weak selectivity and commissions; H3–H5 were blocked by missing infrastructure; root cause was weak candidate architecture, not just wrong hypothesis families.
Codify v2 invariants: base immutable, no raw H1/H2 retries, complement-first for every candidate, single-filter entry banned, model-first banned until Wave A complete.

Define the allowed v2 branches: IV dynamics from iv_est_1, event×state interactions, classifier-transition specialists, cross-symbol leadership (after data readiness), model-backed routing (late wave only).
Create a research_map entry listing Wave A and Wave B hypothesis families with statuses.
success_criteria: Explicit postmortem is recorded and the agent understands v2 is a different research class, not a retry of the same raw ideas.

stages.title: Build base-absent research surface
stages.objective: Construct an anchor-level map that labels every 1h bar as base-long-fired, base-short-fired, or base-absent, then identify subspaces where the base is silent but the market has directional opportunity.
actions: Run an experiment to build an anchor-level table: each 1h anchor, whether base gave a long/short decision, available features on that anchor, and subsequent window outcome for long and short.
Label every anchor as base_long_fired, base_short_fired, or base_absent.
On the base_absent subset, profile distributions of hour, dow, cl_1h, cl_4h, cl_1d, rsi, iv_est, event distances, and weak-quarter/weak-month clusters.
Identify subspaces where the base is silent but the market exhibits directional tendency.
Partition identified subspaces into candidate long specialists, candidate short specialists, and unclear/noisy zones.
success_criteria: A map of missing regimes exists specifically on the surface where the base does not participate.

stages.title: Wave A — low-friction new layers on already available data
stages.objective: Select hypothesis families that can be built without heavy external infrastructure, using already available 1h data and minimal new custom features.
actions: Define hypothesis contracts for each family: A1 IV dynamics from iv_est_1 (rate of change, acceleration, sign reversal, stretch vs recent history), A2 event×state interactions (funding/expiry distance combined with IV dynamics or classifier state), A3 classifier-transition specialists (regime transitions across windows), A4 compression-to-release without models (range compression, low realized mobility, activity decline followed by confirmed release).
For each hypothesis contract specify: target missing regime, why it is not a duplicate of the base, why it should work specifically on the base_absent surface.
For A1 and A2, identify which custom features need to be published.
For A3 and A4, check which features already exist and which need to be added.
Select at most 6–8 hypothesis prototypes total.
success_criteria: A small but high-quality list of Wave A hypotheses exists, all data-ready or nearly data-ready.

stages.title: Feature contract and custom feature publication for Wave A
stages.objective: Translate hypotheses into causally correct features with explicit source, lookback, warm-up behavior, and human-readable interpretation.
actions: For each new feature document: source, causal availability at decision time, lookback window, warm-up behavior, human-readable interpretation.
Design IV dynamics features: IV change over 1h, 3–6h, IV acceleration, IV dynamics reversal, current IV relative to recent mean/median, IV stretch combined with classifier state.
Design classifier transition features capturing state changes between adjacent windows.
Design compression-release features using only past-only compression indicators.
For each custom feature: validate via features_custom, publish via features_custom, rebuild materialized dataset via features_dataset, verify via features_dataset inspect.
Record each feature's human interpretation in research_record.
success_criteria: All Wave A features are published, causality-checked, materialized, and verified.

stages.title: Hypothesis plausibility on base-absent surface
stages.objective: Perform early triage of hypothesis prototypes exclusively on the complement surface where the base is absent, rejecting broad or noisy candidates before building full signals.
actions: For each prototype compute profitability and slice behavior only on the base_absent subset.
Evaluate: observation count, stability across hours and days-of-week, whether the effect is limited to a single extreme bucket.
Seek structured effects, not single lucky tails.
Immediately reject candidates that produce noise, are too broad, or fail to narrow the space to a meaningful specialist regime.
Record rejection reasons in research_record.
success_criteria: Only plausibility-approved prototypes remain, each showing structured effect specifically where the base is absent.

stages.title: Build strict specialist prototypes
stages.objective: Convert surviving ideas into real signal prototypes with at least three logical layers, avoiding the weak single-filter architecture that doomed H1/H2.
actions: For each candidate build 2–4 strict prototypes, each with at least three layers: new information layer, regime/state confirmation, entry-density limiter.
Prohibit wide parameter sweeps.
Pre-register for each prototype: expected specialist regime, why additive by construction, expected trade density, why it should not cannibalize the base.
Reject any prototype that produces hundreds of weak entries without tuning.
Record prototype architecture and rationale in research_record.
success_criteria: A small set of strict specialist prototypes exists, not a loose grid of weak filters.

stages.title: Complement-first standalone backtests
stages.objective: Verify whether each prototype has standalone value specifically on the base-absent surface before any integration testing.
actions: Run standalone backtest in complement-only mode: signal may enter only on base_absent anchor bars.
Only after complement-only passes, run full standalone for overall behavior reference.
Record for each prototype: net PnL, trade count, profit factor, max drawdown, PnL per trade, profit concentration in top N trades, quarterly profile.
Watch specifically for the H1/H2 failure pattern: too many trades, PF near 1.0, fees killing the idea.
success_criteria: A shortlist of prototypes exists that look like real specialist candidates even in complement-only mode.

stages.title: Stability, concentration and specialist verification
stages.objective: Retain only prototypes that work as genuine specialist layers across time, not those riding a single lucky historical stretch.
actions: Run window stability analysis on each complement-approved prototype.
Run condition stability analysis on key conditions via backtests_conditions.
Identify strong and weak windows.
Check whether all profit comes from a single quarter or a single anomalous move.
For specialist-type candidates, prove: which regime they repair, and that this regime was genuinely weak or empty for the base.
success_criteria: Only stable specialists or honestly limited but reproducible regime-repair layers remain.

stages.title: Integration with base in two modes
stages.objective: Test every finalist in both strict-additive and permissive-integrated modes against the unchanged base to separate genuinely additive signals from those that look good only by mixing with base trades.
actions: For each finalist run strict-additive integration: candidate forbidden on any anchor where base already entered.
Run permissive-integrated backtest: normal integration without hard exclusion.
Compare both modes against the unchanged base alone.
Record: delta PnL, delta trades, delta PF, delta DD, weak-quarter repair, difference between strict and permissive modes.
If strict additive is already strong — strong orthogonality signal. If strict is weak but permissive is strong — risk of hidden duplication. If both weak — reject.
success_criteria: A clear picture exists of who is genuinely additive versus who only looks good by mixing with the base.

stages.title: Cannibalization, ownership and new-entry proof
stages.objective: Formally prove that each surviving candidate contributes genuinely new trades and new profit rather than parasitizing on base entries.
actions: For each integration candidate compute: overlap with base, count of truly new trades, count of base trades displaced, PnL of new trades specifically, profit ownership attribution, coverage expansion (which hours/states/event subspaces are now covered).
Reject candidate if: overlap is high and strict additive is weak; new-trade PnL is low; ownership shows the candidate is not a profit carrier; the new layer mainly intercepts good base trades.
success_criteria: Direct proof exists of which candidates genuinely expand the system.

stages.title: Wave B — medium-friction branch
stages.objective: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A failed to produce a conclusive additive candidate.
actions: Verify gate: Wave A is inconclusive — no finalist passed cannibalization and ownership proof.
B1: Check ETHUSDT 1h dataset readiness, build cross-symbol leadership features, test as specialist.
B2: Prepare model training dataset, train compression/routing model, publish as model-backed feature, test as specialist.
B3: Only after B1 and B2 progress — build event-state-model hybrid candidates.
success_criteria: Wave B branches are evaluated with at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.
stages.gate_hint: Wave A inconclusive: no candidate passed both strict-additive integration and cannibalization/ownership proof. Only then is Wave B justified.

stages.title: Walk-forward and final selection
stages.objective: Run walk-forward evaluation on all finalists, produce the final selection report, and give an honest answer on whether a new additive layer was found.
actions: Run walk-forward evaluation on every surviving finalist via backtests_walkforward.
Compare walk-forward performance against the unchanged base: final uplift, drawdown quality, new-entry proof persistence.
Write the final report in research_record: what was found, what was rejected and why, honest assessment of additive value.

Complete the operational checklist: base untouched, no H1/H2 retries, base-absent surface built, all hypotheses documented as genuinely new, complement-first verified, strict-additive and permissive tests done, rejection journal filled, cannibalism journal filled, final report written.
If a valid additive layer was found, snapshot it via backtests_strategy for potential signal-api binding.
success_criteria: Walk-forward results confirm or refute each finalist. Final report gives an honest answer: was a new additive layer found or not.

warnings: Plan is written in Russian; semantic intent was inferred without loss of meaning.
Stage 11 (Wave B) is explicitly gated on Wave A being inconclusive — the compiler must treat it as a conditional branch.
